skill.name: top-web-vulnerabilities
skill.description: Reference skill for identifying common web application vulnerabilities, understanding root causes, assessing impact, and selecting practical mitigations.
skill.summary: Map common web vulnerabilities to causes, impacts, mitigations, and assessment checklists.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 33 potential issues, but the external command, Keychain, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance hits are references inside Markdown documentation, not executable logic. The skill has medium risk because it provides dual-use web security testing guidance, including WAF bypass troubleshooting and out-of-band verification techniques that require authorization boundaries.

medium_findings.title: Dual-Use Web Testing Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill includes troubleshooting advice for WAF blocking using rate adjustment, IP rotation, and payload encoding. It also lists out-of-band DNS/HTTP callbacks and external entity verification techniques. These can support legitimate testing, but they can also enable unauthorized probing if used outside an approved scope.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly describe active verification and evasion-adjacent techniques. The surrounding document is framed as security assessment guidance, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution location is a Markdown code fence containing HTTP security header examples. No executable Ruby, shell command, or command interpolation appears at this location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows a fenced text block of HTTP headers, not a command execution construct. There is no user input flow or executable script context.

low_findings.title: Static Keychain Access Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The Keychain hit appears in a mobile security table as recommended secure storage wording. It does not access macOS Keychain APIs, local credentials, or any sensitive host resource.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is table content that says Keychain or Keystore should be used for encryption. There is no code, command, or API call.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Code Execution Terms Are Reference Content
low_findings.description: The high-severity static hits are terms inside vulnerability names, impacts, mitigations, and mapping tables. They do not implement weak cryptography, execute code, or instruct the assistant to run a dangerous command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review shows these lines are headings, table cells, or descriptive mitigation text. The skill is a Markdown reference with no executable implementation file.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Terms Are Descriptive Reference Content
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance hits describe vulnerability impacts, categories, and assessment topics in a reference catalog. They do not provide scanner commands, target discovery automation, credential harvesting logic, or exfiltration code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of vulnerability definitions or mitigation tables. Some content is dual-use, but these specific static hits are not themselves executable reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Web security reviews need consistent coverage across many vulnerability classes. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured reference for causes, impacts, mitigations, and assessment planning.
actual_capabilities: Explains common web vulnerabilities with definitions, root causes, impacts, and mitigations.
Groups vulnerabilities into categories such as injection, authentication, access control, APIs, SSRF, and client-side risks.
Maps vulnerability areas to OWASP Top 10 categories for planning and reporting.
Provides quick-reference controls for security headers, TLS, authorization, and input validation.
Supports assessment checklist creation for security reviews and remediation planning.
limitations: It is a static reference and does not scan applications by itself.
It does not replace current vulnerability databases or vendor advisories.
Testing advice must be used only on systems where the user has authorization.
Mitigations require adaptation to the application stack and deployment model.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Application Security Review
use_cases.description: Create a checklist that covers major web vulnerability categories before a manual review or penetration test.

use_cases.title: Triage Web Findings
use_cases.description: Classify reported issues by root cause, expected impact, and likely remediation path.

use_cases.title: Teach Secure Development Concepts
use_cases.description: Explain common vulnerability types in simple language for developers who need practical prevention guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Vulnerability
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner learning a single web security concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain cross-site scripting using this skill. Include the root cause, impact, and three practical mitigations.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Review Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning a scoped application security review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a web application security checklist for a public API and admin dashboard. Group checks by vulnerability category.

prompt_templates.title: Triage Findings
prompt_templates.scenario: Prioritizing results from a scanner and manual review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Map these findings to vulnerability categories, estimate likely impact, and recommend remediation priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Remediation Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning across architecture, code, and operations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a phased remediation plan for injection, access control, API, and SSRF risks. Include owners, controls, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Explain IDOR risk for a customer profile endpoint.
output: Root cause: The endpoint trusts a user-supplied object identifier without checking ownership.
Impact: A user may read or change another user profile.
Mitigation: Enforce server-side authorization on every object access and use audit logging.

output_examples.input: Create a short checklist for API security review.
output: Test authorization for horizontal and vertical access control failures.
Review input validation, rate limits, error handling, and key storage.
Confirm transport security and logging coverage.

output_examples.input: Prioritize SQL injection and missing security headers.
output: SQL injection is usually higher priority because it can expose or modify database records.
Missing headers still matter, especially for XSS and clickjacking defense.
Fix injection first, then apply headers as part of baseline hardening.

best_practices: Use the skill to plan authorized reviews with clear scope and written permission.
Pair each identified vulnerability with root cause, business impact, and a testable mitigation.
Validate recommendations against the current application stack and production constraints.
anti_patterns: Do not use testing guidance against systems without explicit authorization.
Do not treat the reference as a live scanner or proof of exploitability.
Do not apply generic mitigations without checking the framework, data flow, and deployment model.

faq.question: Does this skill scan a website automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is a reference and planning aid. A user must provide context, findings, or authorized testing results.

faq.question: Can it help with OWASP Top 10 work?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an OWASP Top 10 mapping and related controls for common web risks.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, if used for learning, design review, or authorized assessment. Some testing topics are dual-use.

faq.question: Can it write remediation guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can explain root causes and propose practical mitigations that should be adapted to the stack.

faq.question: Does it include current CVE data?
faq.answer: No. It is a general vulnerability reference and should be paired with current advisories for product-specific issues.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
